english: to gall, the feet by friction of the shoes W. — 3 to bark W.

1. a dense fog, k̲yim (khyim) fog is coming on, ˚tib ('thib) Cs. id.; saṅs (sangs) has cleared away Cs.; rmúgs-pa-ċan (rmugs pa can) foggy; nam rmúgs-pa (nam rmugs pa) Dzl. 230, 12, a dark, foggy night (another reading: rmús-pa (rmus pa)); Dzl. 100, 15, nyin-mtsán-du yul rmúgs-pa (nyin mtshan du yul rmugs pa) (rmús-pa (rmus pa)), covered with fog, wrapt in darkness. — 2. Sch. eyes heavy with sleep. — 3. inertness, languor, laziness Mil.; inert, languid, sluggish, rmúgs-par byéd-pa (rmugs par byed pa) Dom.
Cs. dull, heavy, stupid; żo rmún-po (zho rmun po) S.g. sour milk (?).
to gnarl and bite each other, of dogs Lex.
v. rmú-ba (rmu ba).

I. to be economizing, parsimonious Lex.; bsris- (bsris-) (Sch. srid?) and sér-rme-ba (ser rme ba) Lex. id. — II. also dmé-ba (dme ba) and smé-ba (sme ba) 1. sbst. spot, speck, mark, a natural mark, on a cane Mil.; mole, mother-spot; *mé-żól* (*me zhol*) W. mark of burning; a detestable sin, esp. murder; uncleanness of food, rme-ytsaṅ-méd (rme gtsang med) or ytsaṅ-rme-méd (gtsang rme med) making no difference as to clean or unclean food Mil.; rme-grib (rme grib) moral defilement; rme-ṡa-ċan (rme sha can) Wdṅ., *me-ṡa za-kan* (*me sha za mkhan*) W., eating unclean flesh, as an animal that devours its own young. — 2. adj., also rmé-ba-ċan (rme ba can), rmé-ċan (rme can) Wdṅ., rmé-po (rme po) Lex. unclean, defiled, contaminated.
n.p. 1. mountain on the Chinese frontier Glr. — 2. a castle in Lhasa Glr.
= ytan (gtan) order, series, row Lex., rmég-méd-pa (rmeg med pa) disordered, not regulated.
crupper, attached to a saddle, sgá-yi rméd (sga yi rmed) Lexx.; góṅ-rmed (gong rmed) Pth.

I. also sméd-pa (smed pa), pf. rmes (rmes), to ask, dri-żiṅ sméd-par mdzád-pa (dri zhing smed par mdzad pa) id. resp. Mil.; snyún-dri sméd-pa (snyun dri smed pa) Mil. = snyún-dri żú-ba (snyun dri zhu ba). — II. to plough and sow; rméd-du jùg-pa (rmed du 'jug pa) to cause to be ploughed and sown, e.g. rice Dzl.
Lex. rmén-bu (rmen bu) Lt., ṡa-rmėn (sha rmen) Mil. and vulgo, gland, swelling of the glands, wen.
1. to pluck out, C., W., Lex., v. bal (bal). — 2. to become threadbare W. — 3. Sch. to appoint, to call, to invite.
Sch. = smre-sṅags (smre sngags).
1. Cs. = ma-ċúṅ (ma chung). — 2. Sch. grandmother.

pf. and imp. rmos (rmos) 1. to plough (up), żiṅ (zhing) frq.; to sow and plough in ˚bras ('bras) Dzl.; ma rmós-pai lo-tóg (ma rmos pa'i lo tog) 1. a fabulous kind of grain in the mythical age. 2. maize, C., W. — rmó-po (rmo po), rmó-mk̲an (rmo mkhan) plough-man. — 2. gyód-rmo-ba (gyod rmo ba) v. gyód-pa (gyod pa).
helmet Glr.; rmog-tsáṅs (rmog tshangs) Cs. 'the padding in a helmet'; k̲rab-rmog (khrab rmog) coat of mail and helmet.

vb. and sbst., pf. rmoṅs (rmongs) to be obscured; obscurity, chiefly in a spiritual sense; also adj. obscured, stultified Stg.; more frq. rmoṅs-pa (rmongs pa), e.g. blo (blo), the mind darkened, by false doctrine Thgy.; by sorrow, despondency, = despairing, despondent, unnerved Dzl., with la (la) or termin., as to, with regard to...; blo ma rmóṅs-pa (blo ma rmongs pa), or rmoṅs-méd (rmongs med) Mil. a mind lively, unimpaired, susceptible, la (la) of; kun-tu-rmóṅ (kun tu rmong) ṡas-ċé-ba (shas che ba) an ample share of irrationality, the principal obstacle to the happiness (ma-k̲óm-pa (ma khom pa)) of those beings which are born as beasts; rmóṅ-par gyúr-ba (rmong par gyur ba) to be obscured, darkened, byéd-pa (byed pa) to obscure, to darken Glr., also: to confound, perplex, deceive, = mgo skór-ba (mgo skor ba) Tar.; rmóṅ-bu (rmong bu) Lex. without expl., Cs.: 'a kind of distemper'; rmóṅ-spu (rmong spu) hair of the abdomen and the pudenda, ra-túg rmoṅ-spus lhog-pa jom (ra thug rmong spus lhog pa 'jom) S.g. the belly-hair of a he-goat tends to heal cancer.
Cs. to plough, rmod-gláṅ (rmod glang) a plough-ox; rmod-lám (rmod lam) Sch. furrow.
1. the act of ploughing; rmón-pa rgyáb-pa (rmon pa rgyab pa) to plough Cs. — 2. a plough-ox, rmon-dór (rmon dor) a yoke of plough-oxen.
S.g. sickness, nausea, k̲ams-rmyá (khams rmya) Lex. id.
